domain_summary: Global geopolitics in early 2026 is dominated by the Russia-Ukraine war entering its fourth year with ongoing territorial disputes over mineral-rich regions valued at $12+ trillion. NATO is undergoing structural realignment toward European leadership while maintaining US commitment, driven by increased defense spending commitments reaching €800 billion by 2030. US-China trade tensions are intensifying under Trump's tariff strategy with a scheduled April 2026 summit, while Middle East conflicts persist despite a Gaza ceasefire framework showing strain. Russia maintains an uncompromising negotiating position demanding five specific Ukrainian regions plus NATO exclusion guarantees, with economic incentives from Donbas coal reserves ($12 trillion value) representing 5-12x Russia's annual GDP.

claims.claim: Russia-Ukraine war enters fourth year with ongoing military operations on day 1,457 as of February 2026
claims.notes: Verified timeline from February 24, 2022 to February 2026

claims.claim: Ukraine achieved fastest territorial gains in over 2 years (201 sq km in Zaporizhzhia) between February 11-15, 2026
claims.notes: Single source reporting, exploiting Starlink outage

claims.claim: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte announced NATO alliance will become more European-led while maintaining US presence (February 13, 2026)
claims.notes: Corroborated by multiple news sources and official NATO statements

claims.claim: NATO member nations increasing defense spending with 3.5% benchmark set for 2035; European defense spending could reach €800 billion by 2030
Trump administration planning April 2026 China visit (March 31-April 2) for trade talks with Xi Jinping
Trump administration using tariffs as central foreign policy tool; Chinese exporters pivoting to developing markets after US tariff increases
Gaza ceasefire began October 10, 2025; escalated Israeli operations in January-February 2026 with highest attacks since ceasefire began
Russia's primary territorial demands focus on five regions: Crimea, Donetsk, Luhansk, Kherson, and Zaporizhzhia
claims.notes: Russia controls approximately 20% of Ukraine's territory

claims.claim: Putin has articulated maximalist territorial claims stating 'the whole of Ukraine is ours' (June 2025), indicating negotiating position extends beyond the five named regions
claims.notes: Contradicts bounded territorial demands, suggests gap between public rhetoric and diplomatic fallback

claims.claim: Donbas contains over 56% of Ukraine's hard coal reserves, valued at approximately $12 trillion
claims.notes: 23 billion tonnes proven reserves, 10 billion tonnes economically extractable

claims.claim: Total Ukraine mineral wealth valued at $26 trillion; Russia controls approximately 70% of this ($12.5+ trillion) following 2022 invasion
claims.notes: Includes coal, critical minerals, and natural gas reserves

claims.claim: Russia's nominal GDP in 2024 was $2.098-2.2 trillion USD; PPP estimates for 2025-2026 range from $7.1-7.8 trillion USD
Russia's official demands include NATO exclusion guarantees and Ukrainian constitutional amendment removing NATO accession goal
Western reporting indicates US has accepted Russian demand for Ukrainian territorial cessions before security guarantees (as of February 13, 2026)
claims.notes: Based on 'Western reporting' characterizations, not confirmed US policy

contradictions.description: Russia's territorial demands appear bounded (five specific regions) versus maximalist claims (entire Ukraine)
contradictions.resolution: Both claims are accurate - represents dual-track strategy with public maximalist rhetoric and bounded negotiating fallback position

knowledge_gaps.gap: Specific US policy position on Ukraine territorial concessions - only 'Western reporting' available, not official US government statements
Details of back-channel communications between Russia and other parties regarding classified territorial settlement terms
Specific USD monetary valuations for Crimea Black Sea natural gas and oil reserves remain unquantified
Detailed breakdown of Donbas mineral value by resource type (lithium, rare earths, specific elements)
Current status of diplomatic negotiations and timeline for potential Russia-Ukraine peace settlement terms
Substantive agenda details for Trump-Xi April 2026 meetings beyond scheduled dates
Gaza ceasefire implementation details and current humanitarian situation metrics
